notes: HOTFIX sprint. Runs in PARALLEL with week-115 (WS-1 money-safety). Triggered by Asterpay dev report 2026-04-17: live-format Invoica API key leaked in public repo github.com/Godman-s/pact/demo-negotiation.ts:42 + PACT verifier.ts HMAC timing attack. Godman-s = founder's own GitHub account (skingem1 / skininthegem@gmail.com) — no external customer notification required. Founder approved revocation 2026-04-17.

tasks.description: Revoke leaked Invoica API key and run forensic audit
tasks.context:
INCIDENT: Key sk_302e3efa383ddf86c2247b7c03f859e6a6b0facab582f5c4be83abea71d17047 was hard-coded in github.com/Godman-s/pact/demo-negotiation.ts:42 and is public. Founder approved revocation 2026-04-17.

Keys are bcrypt-hashed (backend/src/services/api-keys.ts:74-90), so you cannot look up by plaintext via DB index. Procedure:

1. Query all ApiKey rows where customer owner email = 'skininthegem@gmail.com' (and any known internal-owner accounts). For each row, bcrypt.compare the plaintext against keyHash. On match, that row is the leaked key.
2. If no match on founder-owned rows, iterate all ApiKey rows and bcrypt-compare (expensive — log runtime).
3. Revoke the matching ApiKey row: set revoked=true, revokedAt=now, revokedReason='leaked-public-repo-godman-s-pact-2026-04-17'.
4. Forensic audit: pull every request log / invoice creation / webhook delivery where Authorization header matched this key (hint: log the keyId of the row found in step 1, then filter request logs by that keyId). Dump to reports/security/incident-2026-04-17-leaked-key-audit.md with timestamps, IPs, endpoints, and response codes.
5. If any unexpected activity (non-founder IPs, unfamiliar endpoints, abnormal volume), escalate to CEO agent immediately and flag as active compromise.
6. Issue a replacement key to the founder, deliver via founder's Telegram (OWNER_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID) — never via public channel or git.

Acceptance: Revoked ApiKey row visible in Supabase. Forensic report filed. Replacement key delivered to founder. Subsequent auth attempt with sk_302e... returns 401 INVALID_KEY.

tasks.description: Fix PACT verifier timing-attack and strip leaked key from Godman-s/pact repo
Scan all founder-owned public repos for additional leaked Invoica credentials
tasks.context:
Given one key leaked in a founder-owned demo repo, check for others. Scan these public repos owned by skingem1 / Godman-s / any other founder GitHub accounts:
- github.com/Godman-s/pact
- github.com/SkinGem/invoica (should be empty of secrets — M1-SEC-06 will verify repo-root hygiene)
- any other public repos returned by gh api /users/skingem1/repos?type=owner and /users/Godman-s/repos?type=owner

Regex: sk_[a-f0-9]{64} | inv_(live|test)_[A-Za-z0-9_-]{40,} | SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY | X402_SELLER | SK= | API_KEY= with a hex/base64-looking value.

Deliverable: reports/security/external-repo-scan-2026-04-17.md listing each repo, any findings, and whether the finding was remediated.

Acceptance: 100% of the founder's public repos scanned. Any finding triggers a new hotfix task.
